healing-word::healing_or_restoration.recommendedJsonChange: No JSON change needed for represented healing dice and scaling.

healing-word::object_stats_or_damageabil.recommendedJsonChange: Track this spell under HEALING data, not object stats.

hellish-rebuke::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: No JSON change needed for represented save-gated fire damage.

hellish-rebuke::environmental_change.recommendedJsonChange: No environmental runtime field needed for this descriptive visual wording in this goal.

heroism::repeat_save_or_recurring_check.closedReason: Heroism now records the start-of-turn Temporary Hit Point grant as structured Recurring Mechanics and runtime recurringMechanics data.
heroism::repeat_save_or_recurring_check.issue: Heroism grants Temporary Hit Points at the start of each target turn, but runtime JSON stores the recurring start-turn effect only in utility prose.
heroism::repeat_save_or_recurring_check.recommendedTemplateChange: Add recurring healing/temp-HP timing fields for start-of-target-turn grants.
heroism::repeat_save_or_recurring_check.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime recurringEffect data for temporary HP equal to spellcasting ability modifier at the start of each target turn.

heroism::healing_or_restoration.issue: Heroism grants recurring Temporary Hit Points, but runtime JSON does not represent them as healing or temporary HP data.
heroism::healing_or_restoration.recommendedTemplateChange: Add temporary-HP fields that support spellcasting ability modifier amounts and recurring timing.
heroism::healing_or_restoration.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime temporaryHp data with amount spellcasting ability modifier and start-of-turn timing.

heroism::object_stats_or_damageability.issue: The detector matched Temporary Hit Points, but this is a creature buff/healing mechanic rather than object durability.
heroism::object_stats_or_damageability.recommendedTemplateChange: Track under healing/restoration, not object stats.
heroism::object_stats_or_damageability.recommendedJsonChange: Track under temporary HP runtime data, not object stats.

heroism::status_or_state_change.issue: Heroism makes the target immune to the Frightened condition, but runtime JSON stores that immunity only in utility prose.
heroism::status_or_state_change.recommendedTemplateChange: Closed by adding the structured Condition Immunities field and mapping it to defensive condition-immunity runtime data.
heroism::status_or_state_change.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding a DEFENSIVE immunity effect with conditionImmunity: ["Frightened"] while Heroism remains active.
heroism::status_or_state_change.reviewerNote: Closed only for Frightened immunity. Heroism's recurring temporary hit points and end-of-spell temporary-HP cleanup remain open in their own findings.

heroism::conditional_ending.closureReason: Added explicit endCleanup template/runtime fields for spell-end removal of temporary hit points sourced from Heroism; kept Conditional Ending fields not_applicable because this cleanup does not end the spell early.
heroism::conditional_ending.issue: Heroism removes any remaining Temporary Hit Points from this spell when the spell ends, but runtime JSON keeps that cleanup in prose.
heroism::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add end-of-spell cleanup fields for removing temp HP granted by the spell.
heroism::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime cleanup data that removes remaining temporary HP sourced from Heroism when the spell ends.

hex::choice_or_mode.issue: Hex requires choosing one ability for the ability-check disadvantage rider; structured and JSON list options, but the choice is not represented as a typed ability-check modifier.
hex::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add ability-check modifier fields for chosen ability and disadvantage.
hex::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime abilityCheckModifier data tied to the chosen ability instead of relying on generic controlOptions text.

hex::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: Hex's extra damage on attack hits is represented, but the chosen-ability disadvantage on ability checks is only generic utility/control text.

hex::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime abilityCheckModifier data for disadvantage on checks made with the chosen ability.

hex::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime curseTransfer data for 0-HP trigger and later bonus-action retargeting.

hunters-mark::object_stats_or_damageabil.issue: The detector matched 0 Hit Points, but Hunter's Mark uses that as a mark-transfer trigger and does not define object or creature durability.

hunters-mark::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime markTransfer data for 0-HP retargeting and durationScaling thresholds for slots 3-4 and 5+.

ice-knife::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: Ice Knife's primary ranged spell attack and secondary Dexterity save are represented; the secondary save now correctly negates the cold damage instead of halving it.

ice-knife::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing the secondary cold damage saveEffect from half to none.

identify::resource_or_consumption.recommendedJsonChange: Track charge revelation under information/knowledge data instead of resource consumption.

identify::social_or_knowledge_effect.issue: Identify reveals item properties, usage, attunement requirement, charges, active spells, creator spell name, and creature-affecting spells, but runtime JSON stores this as one information description.

identify::social_or_knowledge_effect.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime knowledgePayload data for each information category instead of one prose description.

illusory-script::message_or_communicatio.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime writtenCommunication data for hidden and altered text payloads.

inflict-wounds::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: Inflict Wounds is a Constitution save for half damage in the canonical prose, and structured markdown plus runtime JSON have been corrected from melee-hit modeling to save-gated damage.

inflict-wounds::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by changing the damage condition from hit to Constitution save for half.

jump::conditional_ending.closedReason: Jump's 'until the spell ends' phrase is ordinary duration scoping, not an early-ending trigger. The actual once-per-turn 30-foot jump option with 10-foot movement spend remains open as jump::manual_fixed_jump_option under movement_or_repositioning.
jump::conditional_ending.issue: Jump's 'until the spell ends' is ordinary duration, but the spell also grants a once-per-turn jump option with a movement spend that runtime JSON only stores in prose.
jump::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add movement option fields for once-per-turn jump distance, movement cost, and while-active duration.
jump::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Closed one stale prose mismatch by replacing the old tripled-jump description with the canonical 30-foot jump for 10 feet of movement; add runtime movementOption data for the actual mechanic.

longstrider::movement_or_repositioning.issue: Longstrider increases the target's Speed by 10 feet, but runtime JSON stores this as generic utility prose instead of a movement/speed modifier.

longstrider::movement_or_repositioning.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime speedModifier data for +10 feet while active.

longstrider::conditional_ending.issue: The phrase 'until the spell ends' is ordinary duration scoping for Longstrider's speed increase, not a special early-ending mechanic.

longstrider::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: No conditional-ending JSON change needed beyond duration.

mage-armor::object_stats_or_damageabilit.recommendedJsonChange: No JSON change needed for represented base AC data.

protection-from-evil-and-good::attack_or.recommendedJsonChange: Closed one mismatch by changing defenseType from advantage_on_saves to disadvantage_on_attacks; add separate runtime conditionalSaveAdvantage data for already-active relevant effects.

protection-from-evil-and-good::social_or.issue: The protection prevents possession by specified creature types, but possession immunity is not represented as structured or runtime data.

protection-from-evil-and-good::social_or.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime possessionImmunity data for Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, and Undead sources.

protection-from-evil-and-good::status_or.issue: The target cannot gain Charmed or Frightened from specified creature types, but status immunity is prose-only.

protection-from-evil-and-good::status_or.recommendedJsonChange: Closed by adding a DEFENSIVE immunity effect with conditionImmunity for Charmed and Frightened and attackerFilter creature types for Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, and Undead.

protection-from-evil-and-good::condition.recommendedJsonChange: No conditional-ending JSON change needed beyond duration/concentration.

purify-food-and-drink::created_object_or.issue: The detector matched food and drink objects, but Purify Food and Drink cleanses existing nonmagical food and drink rather than creating objects or structures.
purify-food-and-drink::created_object_or.recommendedTemplateChange: Track the cleansing effect under restoration/environmental or food-and-drink utility fields, not created objects.
purify-food-and-drink::created_object_or.recommendedJsonChange: Track the cleansing effect under purification utility data rather than createdObject data.

purify-food-and-drink::resource_or_consu.issue: Purify Food and Drink removes poison and rot from nonmagical food and drink in an area, but runtime JSON only stores a generic creation utility description.
purify-food-and-drink::resource_or_consu.recommendedTemplateChange: Add food/drink purification fields for contaminant types, nonmagical restriction, and area scope.
purify-food-and-drink::resource_or_consu.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime purification data for poison and rot removal from nonmagical food and drink.

sanctuary::choice_or_mode.issue: A failed save against Sanctuary forces the attacker to choose a new target or lose the attack/spell, but runtime JSON keeps that branch in description text.
sanctuary::choice_or_mode.recommendedTemplateChange: Add failed-save forced-choice fields for choose_new_target_or_lose_action.
sanctuary::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime failedSaveOptions data for choosing a new target or losing the attack/damaging spell.

sanctuary::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: Sanctuary's Wisdom save trigger for creatures targeting the warded creature is represented, but the trigger does not distinguish attack rolls from damaging spells or the area-of-effect bypass.
sanctuary::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add ward targeting-trigger fields for attack roll, damaging spell, and area-of-effect exclusion.
sanctuary::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime wardTrigger data that includes attack rolls and damaging spells but excludes area effects.

sanctuary::ward_alarm_or_trigger.issue: Sanctuary is a ward with incoming targeting triggers and area-effect bypass; current utility effects preserve some behavior but flatten trigger scope and failed-save options.
sanctuary::ward_alarm_or_trigger.recommendedTemplateChange: Add ward fields for protected target, incoming trigger kinds, save, failed-save options, area bypass, and end conditions.
sanctuary::ward_alarm_or_trigger.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime ward data instead of splitting the mechanic across generic utility descriptions.

sanctuary::conditional_ending.closedReason: Sanctuary now records the three canonical spell-ending triggers in structured markdown and runtime conditionalEndings: target_makes_attack_roll, target_casts_spell, and target_deals_damage. The ward trigger/save/fail-choice/area-bypass mechanics remain open in their own buckets.
sanctuary::conditional_ending.issue: Sanctuary ends if the warded creature makes an attack roll, casts a spell, or deals damage, but runtime JSON only has a generic on_target_attack utility description.
sanctuary::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add conditional ending fields for warded target attack roll, spell cast, and damage dealt.
sanctuary::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime ending conditions for target attacks, casts a spell, or deals damage.

searing-smite::attack_or_save_modifier.issue: Searing Smite's recurring Constitution save occurs after start-of-turn fire damage and ends the spell on success; the initial hit now applies the Ignited state without an incorrect initial save, but recurring damage/save sequencing still lacks first-class fields.
searing-smite::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedTemplateChange: Add recurring damage plus post-damage save fields with success_ends semantics.
searing-smite::attack_or_save_modifier.recommendedJsonChange: Closed one mismatch by removing the initial save gate and setting repeatSave to turn_start Constitution; add runtime recurring damage data and explicit damage-then-save sequencing.

searing-smite::repeat_save_or_recurring_.closedReason: Searing Smite now records turn-start Fire damage, Constitution repeat save timing, and damage-then-save sequencing in structured fields and runtime recurringMechanics data.
searing-smite::repeat_save_or_recurring_.issue: The target takes fire damage at the start of each of its turns and then makes a Constitution save; runtime JSON has repeatSave timing but recurring damage remains inside status prose.
searing-smite::repeat_save_or_recurring_.recommendedTemplateChange: Add recurring start-turn damage fields paired with repeat save timing.
searing-smite::repeat_save_or_recurring_.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime recurringDamage data for 1d6 Fire at target turn start, followed by Constitution save to end.

searing-smite::environmental_change.issue: The burning presentation is part of the Ignited damage condition and does not create separate terrain, environmental fire, or object ignition rules.
searing-smite::environmental_change.recommendedTemplateChange: No environmental field needed for this descriptive fire presentation in this goal.
searing-smite::environmental_change.recommendedJsonChange: No environmental runtime field needed beyond the Ignited recurring damage mechanic.

searing-smite::conditional_ending.closedReason: Searing Smite already exposes the repeat Constitution save ending in structured markdown and runtime JSON: Repeat Save Timing is turn_start, Repeat Save Type is Constitution, Repeat Save Success Ends is true, and recurringMechanics records that the start-turn Fire damage happens before the save that can end the spell.
searing-smite::conditional_ending.issue: Searing Smite ends on a successful Constitution save after the recurring start-turn fire damage, but runtime JSON does not make the damage-then-save ending order explicit.
searing-smite::conditional_ending.recommendedTemplateChange: Add conditional ending fields for successful repeat save after start-turn damage.
searing-smite::conditional_ending.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime successEnds data tied to a post-damage turn_start Constitution save.

silent-image::illusion_or_disguise.issue: Silent Image creates a movable image with size/form limits, natural movement appearance, physical-interaction reveal, Study action reveal, Investigation check against spell save DC, and see-through discerned state, but JSON stores this as one sensory utility description.

sleep::choice_or_mode.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime areaTargetSelection data for caster-chosen creatures in the sphere.
